compiled_state.title: red-zone.sh: parse_failing_tests_from_output has no Jest output pattern — RED-zone gate non-functional for Jest files
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Problem

parse_failing_tests_from_output in hooks/lib/red-zone.sh (lines 115-141) supports three output formats (bash, bash assert_pass_if_clean, pytest) but has no grep pattern for Jest failure format (● U+25CF bullet + › U+203A separator). All Jest test failures silently bypass the RED-zone gate.

## Expected Behavior

The parser should extract test names from Jest failure output (● describe › test_name format) and pass them to the RED-zone boundary check.

## Actual Behavior

Parser returns empty array for Jest output. In record-test-status.sh line 962, the empty result triggers the tolerant branch (line 964: 'parser found no matching function names; tolerating as RED-zone failure'), which skips the blocking check entirely. Every Jest failure is tolerated even if outside the RED zone.

## Affected Functions

- parse_failing_tests_from_output (hooks/lib/red-zone.sh:115-141) — missing Jest fail pattern
- parse_passing_tests_from_output (hooks/lib/red-zone.sh:149-171) — missing Jest pass pattern (✓ U+2713)
- get_test_line_number (hooks/lib/red-zone.sh:177-204) — no change needed, word-boundary regex already handles multi-word Jest names

## Reproduction

Any project using .test-index with RED markers on .ts/.tsx test files will silently bypass the RED-zone gate on all Jest failures.

## Severity

P3 — RED-zone gate non-functional for Jest files; overall test gate still blocks on non-zero exit codes when no RED marker present.
